vocabulary.example_cn: 双方同意停火24小时。

vocabulary.en: to fall down or fail suddenly
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump thinks this deal has totally collapsed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 谈判在最后一刻破裂了。

vocabulary.en: something that you give or allow to someone in order to reach an agreement
vocabulary.excerpt: To try and get some nuclear concessions.
vocabulary.example_cn: 作为让步，公司同意提高工资。

vocabulary.en: a time or date by which something must be done
vocabulary.excerpt: Even though they were up against this 60-day deadline.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们必须在截止日期前提交报告。

vocabulary.en: not honest; intending to deceive
vocabulary.excerpt: Furious at Iran for being dishonest negotiators.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被指控有不诚实行为。

vocabulary.en: a ship used for carrying goods or passengers for money
vocabulary.excerpt: Because they were firing on those commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.example_cn: 霍尔木兹海峡的商船遭到袭击。

vocabulary.en: crazy or silly
vocabulary.excerpt: He called them scum. He said that they were cuckoo.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的想法简直疯了。

vocabulary.en: to start again after a pause
vocabulary.excerpt: Suggesting that he's prepared to resume this conflict.
vocabulary.example_cn: 谈判将于下周恢复。

vocabulary.en: a sudden disaster that causes great suffering or damage
vocabulary.excerpt: If the conflict continued, it would result in economic catastrophe.
vocabulary.example_cn: 地震是一场巨大的灾难。

vocabulary.en: a small sign of hope
vocabulary.excerpt: Although the president does say that there's a glimmer of hope that these negotiations can continue.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在黑暗中仍有一线希望。

vocabulary.en: intended to make someone less angry or more willing to compromise
vocabulary.excerpt: Perhaps a more conciliatory president would come here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他发表了和解性的讲话。

vocabulary.en: a complaint or a feeling of being treated unfairly
vocabulary.excerpt: All of his grievances and all of his frustrations at NATO have not waned one bit.
vocabulary.example_cn: 工人们表达了对工作条件的不满。

vocabulary.en: in a way that is too large or too small compared to something else
vocabulary.excerpt: We pay disproportionately.
vocabulary.example_cn: 少数人承担了不成比例的负担。

vocabulary.en: to increase something gradually
vocabulary.excerpt: I think President Trump is just trying to ratchet up the visible frustration.
vocabulary.example_cn: 政府正在逐步加大对对手的压力。

vocabulary.en: a system or method of controlling something
vocabulary.excerpt: Where they can gradually establish a regime of control.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们试图建立一套严格的控制体系。

vocabulary.en: to become greater or more serious
vocabulary.excerpt: See whether the U.S. really wants to escalate this or not.
vocabulary.example_cn: 冲突可能升级为全面战争。

vocabulary.en: a set of strategies or tactics used by someone
vocabulary.excerpt: That is the playbook here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他遵循了标准的竞选策略手册。

vocabulary.en: permission to start or continue something
vocabulary.excerpt: Does that mean Israel still has a kind of green light to continue its fight?
vocabulary.example_cn: 项目已获绿灯放行。

vocabulary.en: a very difficult or complicated problem
vocabulary.excerpt: It is at the center of this kind of Gordian knot of international issues.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个外交僵局就像戈尔迪之结一样难解。

vocabulary.en: the act of managing multiple tasks or problems at the same time
vocabulary.excerpt: There's a lot of spinning plates here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 作为CEO，他必须同时处理许多事务。

vocabulary.en: to be different or to develop in a different direction
vocabulary.excerpt: There are few cases where they diverge.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们的观点在关键问题上存在分歧。

vocabulary.en: to withdraw from a position or stop doing something
vocabulary.excerpt: I think they're going to stand down on any attacks for a little while.
vocabulary.example_cn: 抗议者被要求停止行动。

vocabulary.en: an election in which voters choose the candidates from each party
vocabulary.excerpt: Ahead of their primary, which is on August 4th.
vocabulary.example_cn: 初选将于下月举行。

vocabulary.en: an attempt to take control of a country by force
vocabulary.excerpt: A progressive insurgency.
vocabulary.example_cn: 该地区爆发了武装叛乱。

vocabulary.en: the people in positions of power in a society or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: More moderate establishment party forces.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他挑战了政治体制。

vocabulary.en: a person who works very hard and reliably
vocabulary.excerpt: A bipartisan, workhorse version of things.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她是团队中的骨干。

vocabulary.en: the largest group in a legislature, or the number of votes by which a person or party wins
vocabulary.excerpt: There is no way that anybody really can see that Democrats can take the majority of the Senate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 共和党在参议院占多数。

vocabulary.en: a state where the outcome of an election is uncertain and both parties campaign heavily
vocabulary.excerpt: Michigan is a battleground state in 2028.
vocabulary.example_cn: 俄亥俄州是总统大选的关键战场州。

vocabulary.en: a situation where many people cannot afford basic necessities like housing or healthcare
vocabulary.excerpt: Both candidates are talking about the affordability crisis that Republicans have created.
vocabulary.example_cn: 许多城市正面临住房可负担性危机。

vocabulary.en: financial assistance provided under the Affordable Care Act to help people pay for health insurance
vocabulary.excerpt: We're going to pass those ACA subsidies that Republicans refused to pass.
vocabulary.example_cn: 数百万美国人依赖ACA补贴来支付医疗保险。

vocabulary.en: to make something turbulent or cause trouble
vocabulary.excerpt: It is an issue that is roiling the Democratic Party right now.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这场丑闻搅乱了政坛。

vocabulary.en: to successfully navigate a difficult situation between two extremes
vocabulary.excerpt: How do you thread the needle and navigate a conversation around this?
vocabulary.example_cn: 他必须在两派之间巧妙周旋。

vocabulary.en: to cause great trouble or problems for someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: A conversation around this that is bedeviling both parties.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个问题长期困扰着该地区。

vocabulary.en: to support or strengthen something that is weak or in danger
vocabulary.excerpt: Construction crews working through the night, because they need to shore up a high rise.
vocabulary.example_cn: 工人们正在加固地基。

vocabulary.en: to bend or become twisted, often due to pressure
vocabulary.excerpt: Two columns buckled on the 21st floor.
vocabulary.example_cn: 桥梁在重压下弯曲了。

vocabulary.en: to move people from a dangerous place to a safer one
vocabulary.excerpt: The building was evacuated after two columns bent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 当局下令疏散该地区。

vocabulary.en: something done to prevent something dangerous or unpleasant from happening
vocabulary.excerpt: Several other buildings were also cleared out as a precaution.
vocabulary.example_cn: 作为预防措施，他们关闭了道路。

vocabulary.en: the act of damaging someone's reputation by saying or writing bad things about them
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump's $3.8 billion defamation lawsuit against "The Washington Post," now thrown out by a judge.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统对《华盛顿邮报》提起的38亿美元诽谤诉讼被法官驳回。

vocabulary.en: claiming or asserting something without proof
vocabulary.excerpt: The president's media and technology group brought the suit in 2023 over a "Post" article alleging securities fraud.
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统的媒体科技集团于2023年因一篇指控证券欺诈的《邮报》文章提起诉讼。

vocabulary.en: the intention to do harm or cause suffering
vocabulary.excerpt: But a federal judge in Florida ruled that Trump media failed to present evidence of actual malice.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但佛罗里达州的一名联邦法官裁定，特朗普媒体未能提供实际恶意的证据。

vocabulary.en: shocked or surprised someone very much
vocabulary.excerpt: Defending champion Argentina stunned Egypt, scoring three second half goals to come back from a two-goal deficit.
vocabulary.example_cn: 卫冕冠军阿根廷队震惊了埃及队，在下半场连进三球，从两球落后的局面中逆转。

vocabulary.en: the amount by which something is less than what is needed or expected
vocabulary.excerpt: Defending champion Argentina stunned Egypt, scoring three second half goals to come back from a two-goal deficit.
vocabulary.example_cn: 卫冕冠军阿根廷队震惊了埃及队，在下半场连进三球，从两球落后的局面中逆转。

vocabulary.en: a method of deciding a tied game in soccer where players take penalty kicks
vocabulary.excerpt: Switzerland took down Colombia in a penalty kick shootout after the game ended in a tie.
vocabulary.example_cn: 瑞士队在比赛打平后通过点球大战击败了哥伦比亚队。

vocabulary.en: the round of a competition before the semi-finals
vocabulary.excerpt: And it will be the first time the Swiss will have reached the World Cup quarter finals since 1954.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这将是瑞士队自1954年以来首次进入世界杯四分之一决赛。

vocabulary.en: the act of officially suggesting someone for a position or award
vocabulary.excerpt: And today the nominations will be announced for the 78th Emmy Awards.
vocabulary.example_cn: 今天将公布第78届艾美奖的提名名单。

vocabulary.en: an agreement to stop fighting for a period of time
vocabulary.excerpt: That's where the president has just said that the ceasefire with Iran is over.
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统刚刚表示与伊朗的停火已经结束。

vocabulary.en: a document that describes the general principles of an agreement between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump saying that the memorandum of understanding with Iran is over.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统表示与伊朗的谅解备忘录已经终止。

vocabulary.en: a temporary agreement to stop fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: So that means this truce is uncertain right now.
vocabulary.example_cn: 所以这意味着目前的休战状态不确定。

vocabulary.en: a sudden increase in violence or activity
vocabulary.excerpt: It follows a flare-up overnight between the U.S. and Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 此前美国与伊朗之间一夜之间爆发了冲突。

vocabulary.en: very severe or overwhelming
vocabulary.excerpt: And Iran promising a crushing response.
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗承诺将做出压倒性的回应。

vocabulary.en: crazy or silly
vocabulary.excerpt: There's something wrong with them. They're cuckoo.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们有问题。他们疯了。

vocabulary.en: people who try to bring about an agreement between opposing sides
vocabulary.excerpt: And what we've seen in the past is the Qatari and Pakistani mediators who are in the middle of this, working diligently, slowly behind the scenes.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们过去看到的是卡塔尔和巴基斯坦的调解人参与其中，在幕后勤奋而缓慢地工作。

vocabulary.en: to reduce the intensity of a conflict or dangerous situation
vocabulary.excerpt: trying to talk both sides down, trying to de-escalate at times
vocabulary.example_cn: 试图说服双方冷静下来，有时试图缓和局势。

vocabulary.en: the effort of diplomacy to achieve something
vocabulary.excerpt: eventually getting that MOU after almost two months of hard diplomatic lifting.
vocabulary.example_cn: 经过近两个月艰苦的外交努力，最终达成了那份谅解备忘录。

vocabulary.en: warfare between forces of unequal strength, often involving unconventional tactics
vocabulary.excerpt: they've become the leading kind of battlefield experts on asymmetrical warfare and drones.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们已成为非对称战争和无人机领域的顶尖战场专家。

vocabulary.en: missiles that are guided in the first part of flight and then fall freely
vocabulary.excerpt: And President Zelenskyy is desperate to get more air defense missiles to stop the big, ballistic missiles that are killing people in Ukraine.
vocabulary.example_cn: 泽连斯基总统迫切希望获得更多防空导弹，以阻止那些在乌克兰造成人员伤亡的大型弹道导弹。

vocabulary.en: something that takes attention away from what you are doing
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran, Israel war has been a distraction, consuming the very resources that Ukraine really needs.
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗、以色列战争一直是一种干扰，消耗了乌克兰真正需要的资源。

vocabulary.en: the act of moving away from what is normal or expected
vocabulary.excerpt: this will be a deviation off the track of what NATO really wants to focus on today.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这将偏离北约今天真正想要关注的重点。

vocabulary.en: the most advanced stage of development
vocabulary.excerpt: he is at the cutting edge, that drone technology changes quickly on the battlefield.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他处于前沿，无人机技术在战场上变化很快。

vocabulary.en: unwanted sexual contact or behavior
vocabulary.excerpt: This is after a new sexual assault allegation against the democratic candidate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是在针对民主党候选人的新的性侵犯指控之后。

vocabulary.en: a pattern of behavior that dominates and controls another person through fear and intimidation
vocabulary.excerpt: coercive control, lying, deception, abuse, and really horrible things
vocabulary.example_cn: 强制控制、撒谎、欺骗、虐待以及非常可怕的事情。

vocabulary.en: to withdraw from a race or competition
vocabulary.excerpt: And Platner is facing widespread calls to drop out of the race from the Democratic Party.
vocabulary.example_cn: 普拉特纳面临民主党广泛要求他退出竞选的呼声。

vocabulary.en: to resign from a position
vocabulary.excerpt: I have recommended that he step down.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我建议他辞职。

vocabulary.en: to remove oneself from a situation or competition
vocabulary.excerpt: he has to withdraw by the 13th.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他必须在13日之前退出。

vocabulary.en: an empty position or place
vocabulary.excerpt: the secretary of state cannot allow a new name on the ballot until there's a vacancy.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在出现空缺之前，州务卿不能允许新名字出现在选票上。

vocabulary.en: gathered closely together, often in a private or secret manner
vocabulary.excerpt: he's spending his time huddled in private trying to figure out what to do.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他正私下里聚在一起，试图弄清楚该怎么做。

vocabulary.en: very enthusiastic and eager
vocabulary.excerpt: a lot of Democrats who were very gung ho about him
vocabulary.example_cn: 很多对他非常狂热的民主党人。

vocabulary.en: dismissed or ignored
vocabulary.excerpt: the previous allegations from women that were all waved off and said it's not enough to be an issue for him
vocabulary.example_cn: 之前来自女性的指控都被挥手拒绝，并说这不足以成为他的问题。

vocabulary.en: a convention that is organized quickly and unexpectedly
vocabulary.excerpt: Should the party have a pop-up convention on the weekend of the 25th
vocabulary.example_cn: 该党是否应该在25日那个周末举行一次临时大会？

vocabulary.en: a meeting of members of a political party to choose candidates or decide policy
vocabulary.excerpt: should they have a statewide caucus, basically redo the primary election?
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们是否应该举行全州范围的党团会议，基本上重新进行初选？

vocabulary.en: evidence that a person is still alive, often demanded in hostage situations
vocabulary.excerpt: And yet some MAGA voices demanding, quote, "proof of life."
vocabulary.example_cn: 然而一些MAGA声音要求所谓的“生命证明”。

vocabulary.en: a machine that helps a person breathe
vocabulary.excerpt: If he's dead or if he's on a ventilator, if he's not able to do his job, he should resign right now.
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果他死了或者靠呼吸机维持生命，如果他无法履行职责，他应该立即辞职。

vocabulary.en: an election held to fill a vacancy that occurs between regular elections
vocabulary.excerpt: They should have a special election.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们应该举行一次特别选举。

vocabulary.en: manipulated someone into questioning their own reality or sanity
vocabulary.excerpt: But we were gaslit and told that he was at the top of his game.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我们被煤气灯效应操纵，被告知他处于最佳状态。

vocabulary.en: causing someone to carry a heavy load or worry
vocabulary.excerpt: I never liked burdening others with my problems.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我从来不喜欢用我的问题给别人增加负担。

sentences.en: The comments this morning from President Trump come after the U.S. military hit more than 80 Iranian targets on Tuesday and, in its biggest move yet, reimposed sanctions on Iranian oil in retaliation for attacks on ships near the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统今早的言论是在美军周二打击了80多个伊朗目标，并采取了迄今最大规模的行动，即重新对伊朗石油实施制裁，以报复霍尔木兹海峡附近船只遭袭之后发表的。
sentences.structure: 主句（The comments...come after...）+ 时间状语从句（after the U.S. military hit...and reimposed...）+ 目的状语（in retaliation for...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为'The comments...come after...'，after引导时间状语从句，从句中并列谓语'hit'和'reimposed'，'in its biggest move yet'为插入语，'in retaliation for...'为目的状语。

sentences.en: But what that actually means for the negotiations and for the war are a little bit unclear.
sentences.cn: 但这实际上对谈判和战争意味着什么，还不太清楚。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what that actually means...）+ 系动词（are）+ 表语（unclear）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主语从句作主语的句子，'what that actually means for the negotiations and for the war'是主语从句，谓语动词用单数形式，但这里用了'are'，可能是口语中的非正式用法。

sentences.en: You know, the president said that he would allow the negotiators to continue these discussions with Iran to try and get some nuclear concessions, most accounts suggest that those talks had been very, very slow going, even though they were up against this 60-day deadline.
sentences.cn: 你知道，总统说他将允许谈判代表继续与伊朗进行这些讨论，试图获得一些核让步；大多数报道表明，这些谈判进展非常非常缓慢，尽管他们面临着60天的最后期限。
sentences.structure: 主句（the president said that...）+ 宾语从句（that he would allow...）+ 并列句（most accounts suggest that...）+ 让步状语从句（even though...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句，第一个分句包含宾语从句，第二个分句包含宾语从句和让步状语从句。'to try and get'是目的状语。

sentences.en: But the president sort of shrugging that off as a waste of time and really entering this summit furious at Iran for being dishonest negotiators, in his view, because they were firing on those commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 但总统有点不以为然，认为这是浪费时间，并且真的带着对伊朗的愤怒进入峰会，在他看来，伊朗是不诚实的谈判者，因为他们向霍尔木兹海峡的那些商船开火。
sentences.structure: 主句（the president...shrugging...and entering...）+ 原因状语（for being...）+ 插入语（in his view）+ 原因状语从句（because...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，但包含多个修饰成分。'shrugging that off'和'entering this summit'是并列的现在分词短语作谓语，'furious at Iran'是形容词短语作伴随状语，'for being dishonest negotiators'是原因状语，'in his view'是插入语，'because...'是原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And so, this is all, I think, falling apart rather quickly, although the president does say that there's a glimmer of hope that these negotiations can continue.
sentences.cn: 所以，我认为这一切都在相当快地瓦解，尽管总统确实说还有一线希望，这些谈判可以继续。
sentences.structure: 主句（this is...falling apart）+ 插入语（I think）+ 让步状语从句（although the president does say that...）+ 同位语从句（that these negotiations can continue）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为'this is...falling apart'，'I think'为插入语，although引导让步状语从句，从句中包含宾语从句'that there's a glimmer of hope'，而'that these negotiations can continue'是'glimmer of hope'的同位语从句。

sentences.en: But it was evident, listening to him this morning, that all of his grievances and all of his frustrations at NATO have not waned one bit.
sentences.cn: 但今早听他讲话，很明显，他对北约的所有不满和沮丧丝毫没有减弱。
sentences.structure: 形式主语（It）+ 系动词（was）+ 表语（evident）+ 插入语（listening to him this morning）+ 真正主语从句（that all of his grievances...have not waned）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个it作形式主语的句子，真正的主语是that引导的主语从句，'listening to him this morning'是现在分词短语作插入语。

sentences.en: And it is an issue that is roiling the Democratic Party right now, and in Michigan especially, there is a large Arab American population, there's a large Jewish population, there is a lot of different views of what should be done there.
sentences.cn: 这是一个目前正在搅动民主党的问题，尤其是在密歇根州，那里有大量的阿拉伯裔美国人和犹太裔美国人，对于应该采取什么行动存在许多不同观点。
sentences.structure: 主句（it is an issue that...）+ 定语从句（that is roiling...）+ 并列句（and in Michigan especially, there is...there is...there is...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句，第一个分句包含定语从句修饰'issue'，第二个分句是三个there be句型并列，'of what should be done there'是介词短语修饰'views'。

sentences.en: El-Sayed has said over and over that he is anti-AIPAC, and he said to me in an interview I did with him last week that the only way that anybody could support Israel is if there's money supporting them.
sentences.cn: 埃尔-赛义德一再表示他反对AIPAC，他在上周我对他的一次采访中告诉我，任何人支持以色列的唯一方式就是有钱支持他们。
sentences.structure: 主句（El-Sayed has said...that...）+ 并列句（and he said to me...that...）+ 定语从句（I did with him last week）+ 表语从句（that the only way...is if...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句，第一个分句包含宾语从句，第二个分句包含宾语从句，其中'that the only way...is if...'是表语从句，'I did with him last week'是定语从句修饰'interview'。

sentences.en: And I think that is what he's going to do with the Iranians here, step by step: keep putting the pressure on about the strait, keep putting pressure on about the nuclear issue, try to get to something that he can actually live with, which is no fighting, so that oil prices and gasoline prices stay down.
sentences.cn: 我认为这就是他在这里要对伊朗人采取的做法，一步一步来：继续就海峡问题施压，继续就核问题施压，试图达成一个他真正能接受的结果，即没有战斗，这样油价和汽油价格就能保持低位。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think that...）+ 表语从句（that is what...）+ 同位语（step by step）+ 并列祈使句（keep putting...keep putting...try to get...）+ 定语从句（that he can actually live with）+ 非限制性定语从句（which is no fighting）+ 目的状语从句（so that...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句为'I think that...'，that引导表语从句，冒号后是三个并列的祈使句，其中'try to get'后接定语从句和目的状语从句。

sentences.en: And in the minds -- whether or not those things are even at odds.
sentences.cn: 而在思想上——这些东西是否甚至相互矛盾。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（in the minds）+ 插入语（whether or not...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个不完整的句子，可能是口语中的省略。'whether or not those things are even at odds'是名词性从句作插入语。

sentences.en: President Trump's $3.8 billion defamation lawsuit against "The Washington Post," now thrown out by a judge.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统对《华盛顿邮报》提起的38亿美元诽谤诉讼，现已被法官驳回。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语是President Trump's $3.8 billion defamation lawsuit against "The Washington Post"，谓语是now thrown out by a judge。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，主语较长，包含所有格和介词短语。谓语部分使用被动语态，表示诉讼被驳回。

sentences.en: Defending champion Argentina stunned Egypt, scoring three second half goals to come back from a two-goal deficit.
sentences.cn: 卫冕冠军阿根廷队震惊了埃及队，在下半场连进三球，从两球落后的局面中逆转。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语是Defending champion Argentina，谓语是stunned，宾语是Egypt，现在分词短语scoring...作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明阿根廷队如何震惊埃及队。不定式短语to come back...表示目的。

sentences.en: And it will be the first time the Swiss will have reached the World Cup quarter finals since 1954.
sentences.cn: 这将是瑞士队自1954年以来首次进入世界杯四分之一决赛。
sentences.structure: 主句是it will be the first time，后面跟一个定语从句the Swiss will have reached...，省略了关系词that。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个固定句型，表示“这是第一次...”。从句使用将来完成时，强调到将来某个时间点已经完成的动作。

sentences.en: President Trump saying that the memorandum of understanding with Iran is over.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统表示与伊朗的谅解备忘录已经终止。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语作伴随状语，主语是President Trump，谓语是saying，后面跟that引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个现在分词短语，在新闻中常用于表示某人正在说的话。宾语从句是系表结构。

sentences.en: It follows a flare-up overnight between the U.S. and Iran.
sentences.cn: 此前美国与伊朗之间一夜之间爆发了冲突。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语是It，谓语是follows，宾语是a flare-up overnight between the U.S. and Iran。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，it指代前文提到的总统声明。follows表示时间上的先后顺序。

sentences.en: And what we've seen in the past is the Qatari and Pakistani mediators who are in the middle of this, working diligently, slowly behind the scenes, trying to talk both sides down, trying to de-escalate at times, getting the original ceasefire, eventually getting that MOU after almost two months of hard diplomatic lifting.
sentences.cn: 我们过去看到的是卡塔尔和巴基斯坦的调解人参与其中，在幕后勤奋而缓慢地工作，试图说服双方冷静下来，有时试图缓和局势，达成最初的停火，经过近两个月艰苦的外交努力，最终达成了那份谅解备忘录。
sentences.structure: 主句是what we've seen in the past is the Qatari and Pakistani mediators，后面跟who引导的定语从句，从句中多个现在分词短语作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复杂句，主语是what引导的主语从句。定语从句修饰mediators，其中包含一系列现在分词短语，描述调解人的一系列行动。

sentences.en: But I think putting it in the context of the NATO summit here, you know, the secretary general, Mark Rutte, said he expected it to be a topic of conversation.
sentences.cn: 但我认为，将其放在北约峰会的背景下，你知道，秘书长马克·吕特表示他预计这将是一个话题。
sentences.structure: 主句是I think，后面跟省略that的宾语从句。宾语从句中，putting it in the context...是现在分词短语作状语，主句是the secretary general...said...。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，think后接宾语从句。现在分词短语putting...作状语，表示条件或背景。said后接省略that的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And over the past few months there's a sense in Europe that the U.S., Iran, Israel war has been a distraction, consuming the very resources that Ukraine really needs, missile defense systems, and that this will be a deviation off the track of what NATO really wants to focus on today.
sentences.cn: 在过去几个月里，欧洲有一种感觉，认为美国、伊朗、以色列战争一直是一种干扰，消耗了乌克兰真正需要的资源，即导弹防御系统，并且这将偏离北约今天真正想要关注的重点。
sentences.structure: 主句是there's a sense in Europe，后面跟两个that引导的同位语从句，由and连接。第一个同位语从句中，consuming...是现在分词短语作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复杂句，there be句型后接同位语从句。第一个同位语从句中包含现在分词短语作状语，第二个同位语从句是系表结构。

sentences.en: And we saw President Zelenskyy pretty much when Iran began hitting the United States' friends and allies in the gulf, he went out to Saudi, he went out to others in the gulf and said, we've got these defensive capabilities against drones.
sentences.cn: 我们看到泽连斯基总统，当伊朗开始打击美国在海湾地区的朋友和盟友时，他前往沙特，前往海湾其他国家，并说，我们有这些针对无人机的防御能力。
sentences.structure: 主句是we saw President Zelenskyy，后面跟when引导的时间状语从句，主句是he went out...and said...，said后跟直接引语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，saw后接宾语，when引导时间状语从句。主句包含并列谓语went out和said，直接引语是简单句。

sentences.en: And that's, you know, where he -- the card that he's short of.
sentences.cn: 这就是，你知道，他缺少的那张牌。
sentences.structure: 主句是that's，后面跟where引导的表语从句，其中包含一个定语从句that he's short of修饰the card。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，where引导表语从句，从句中the card是主语，that he's short of是定语从句。

sentences.en: The things that he does in private, in his relationships with women, the things that he has done to women, coercive control, lying, deception, abuse, and really horrible things that we know about him -- sorry, we know that, that you can't be that way in private and not have it translate into how you would govern.
sentences.cn: 他在私下里、在与女性的关系中做的事情，他对女性做过的事情，强制控制、撒谎、欺骗、虐待以及我们知道的非常可怕的事情——抱歉，我们知道，你不能在私下里那样做而不让它转化为你将如何治理。
sentences.structure: 这是一个复杂句，主语是The things...，谓语是隐含的are或类似动词，但实际句子结构不完整。后面跟that引导的定语从句，以及并列的名词短语。最后是that引导的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个口语化的句子，结构松散。开头是名词短语作主语，后面跟一系列列举。最后一部分是that引导的宾语从句，其中包含not...and...结构。

sentences.en: And even though people keep saying to him, no, no, no, no, no, he is saying, yes, I can keep doing this, right.
sentences.cn: 尽管人们一直对他说，不，不，不，不，不，他却在说，是的，我可以继续这样做，对吧。
sentences.structure: 主句是he is saying，前面是even though引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，even though引导让步状语从句。主句和从句都包含直接引语。

sentences.en: I think it's really important that as many Democratic voters have a voice in this process when Graham Platner withdraws.
sentences.cn: 我认为，当格雷厄姆·普拉特纳退出时，尽可能多的民主党选民在这个过程有发言权是非常重要的。
sentences.structure: 主句是I think，后面跟省略that的宾语从句。宾语从句中，it是形式主语，真正的主语是that引导的主语从句，其中包含when引导的时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，think后接宾语从句。宾语从句中使用it作形式主语，that从句是真正主语，其中包含时间状语从句。

sentences.en: We have an obligation, as -- in service of not just Mainers, but in service of the entire country to defeat Senator Collins this upcoming November.
sentences.cn: 我们有义务，不仅为缅因州人民服务，而且为整个国家服务，在今年11月击败参议员柯林斯。
sentences.structure: 主句是We have an obligation，后面跟不定式短语to defeat...作定语，as引导的插入语表示方式。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，不定式短语作定语修饰obligation。as引导的插入语补充说明义务的内容。

sentences.en: And I think there's a lesson for Democrats to learn in these moments.
sentences.cn: 我认为在这些时刻民主党有一个教训要吸取。
sentences.structure: 主句是I think，后面跟省略that的宾语从句。宾语从句是there be句型，不定式短语to learn作定语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，think后接宾语从句。there be句型中，不定式短语修饰lesson。

sentences.en: But the lesson for some Democrats was what was going on until Monday with Platner, which is to say all of those things that are supposed to be problems we're going to say aren't problems and we should go forward here.
sentences.cn: 但对一些民主党人来说，教训是直到周一普拉特纳发生的事情，也就是说，所有那些本应是问题的事情，我们都会说不是问题，我们应该继续前进。
sentences.structure: 主句是the lesson...was，后面跟what引导的表语从句。表语从句中，which is to say引导一个解释性的从句，其中包含that引导的定语从句和并列句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复杂句，what引导表语从句。which is to say是非限制性定语从句，修饰整个表语从句。其中包含that定语从句修饰things，以及并列句we're going to say...and we should go...。

sentences.en: And it's a difference in how Democrats and Republicans operate for sure, whether it's Ken Paxton or Max Miller or there are a lot of -- or Donald Trump, there are a lot of people --
sentences.cn: 这当然是民主党和共和党运作方式的差异，无论是肯·帕克斯顿还是马克斯·米勒，还是有很多——或者唐纳德·特朗普，有很多人——
sentences.structure: 主句是it's a difference，后面跟in how引导的介词宾语从句。whether...or...引导让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，how引导宾语从句作介词in的宾语。whether...or...引导让步状语从句，列举例子。

sentences.en: And I just think that from the party who claims transparency and accountability, we should just know more when these situations pop up.
sentences.cn: 我只是认为，从声称透明和问责的政党那里，当这些情况出现时，我们应该知道更多。
sentences.structure: 主句是I just think，后面跟that引导的宾语从句。宾语从句中，from the party...是状语，who claims...是定语从句修饰party，when引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，think后接宾语从句。宾语从句中包含定语从句和时间状语从句。

sentences.en: If this were Democrats, Republicans would be wetting their diapers about accountability and responsibility.
sentences.cn: 如果这是民主党人，共和党人就会在问责和责任问题上尿裤子。
sentences.structure: 这是一个虚拟条件句，if引导非真实条件句，主句用would be doing。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个与现在事实相反的虚拟语气，条件从句用were，主句用would be doing，表示假设情况。

sentences.en: But I've learned from this experience.
sentences.cn: 但我从这次经历中学到了。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语是I，谓语是've learned，介词短语from this experience作状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，现在完成时表示过去的经历对现在的影响。

sentences.en: Taking this kind of job means losing some of the privacy that most of us expect.
sentences.cn: 接受这种工作意味着失去我们大多数人期望的一些隐私。
sentences.structure: 主语是动名词短语Taking this kind of job，谓语是means，宾语是动名词短语losing some of the privacy，后面跟that引导的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，动名词短语作主语和宾语。定语从句修饰privacy。

sentences.en: The American people have a right to know if their leaders are facing health challenges that might affect their ability to perform their duties, even temporarily.
sentences.cn: 美国人民有权知道他们的领导人是否面临可能影响其履行职责能力的健康挑战，即使是暂时的。
sentences.structure: 主句是The American people have a right to know，后面跟if引导的宾语从句。宾语从句中，that引导定语从句修饰health challenges。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，不定式短语to know后接if引导的宾语从句。定语从句修饰challenges。

topics.title: 美伊关系与核协议
topics.content: 特朗普总统宣布与伊朗的谅解备忘录（MOU）结束，美军打击了80多个伊朗目标并重新实施石油制裁，伊朗威胁将做出“毁灭性回应”。这标志着美伊紧张局势升级，核谈判面临破裂。背景包括伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡袭击商船、美国要求伊朗停止核活动等。
topics.keywords: Iran, nuclear deal, sanctions, Strait of Hormuz, MOU

topics.title: 北约峰会与特朗普的批评
topics.content: 特朗普在土耳其安卡拉参加北约峰会，对北约盟友表达不满，认为美国承担了不成比例的防务费用，并批评盟友在需要时未提供支持。这反映了特朗普一贯对北约的批评态度，以及北约内部关于军费分摊的长期矛盾。

topics.title: 密歇根州民主党参议员初选
topics.content: 密歇根州民主党参议员初选在Abdul El-Sayed和Haley Stevens之间展开，两人在以色列政策、AIPAC资金、党内路线等问题上激烈交锋。该初选被视为民主党内部进步派与建制派斗争的缩影，对2026年中期选举和2028年大选具有风向标意义。
topics.keywords: Michigan, Democratic primary, Senate, AIPAC, progressive vs establishment

topics.title: 纽约曼哈顿高层建筑结构问题
topics.content: 纽约曼哈顿一栋前辉瑞总部大楼在改建为公寓时，两根柱子发生弯曲，导致建筑疏散。施工人员连夜进行加固。该事件引发对建筑安全和施工监管的关注。
topics.keywords: New York, high-rise, structural failure, evacuation, construction safety

topics.title: 美伊关系与核协议
topics.content: 特朗普总统宣布终止与伊朗的谅解备忘录，美伊之间再次爆发冲突。美国打击了80个目标，伊朗承诺做出回应。此前卡塔尔和巴基斯坦曾进行调解，但特朗普表示不再与伊朗谈判。
topics.keywords: Iran, ceasefire, memorandum of understanding, nuclear weapon, mediators

topics.title: 北约峰会与乌克兰局势
topics.content: 北约峰会在安卡拉举行，焦点是乌克兰问题。泽连斯基寻求更多防空导弹以应对俄罗斯的弹道导弹。欧洲认为美伊冲突分散了资源，影响了乌克兰的防御。
topics.keywords: NATO summit, Ukraine, Zelenskyy, air defense missiles, ballistic missiles

topics.title: 美国参议院选举与格雷厄姆·普拉特纳丑闻
topics.content: 民主党候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳面临新的性侵犯指控，包括前女友的暴力指控。党内要求他退选，但他在考虑是否退出以及如何指定继任者。缅因州参议院席位竞争激烈，民主党担心失去机会。
topics.keywords: Graham Platner, sexual assault, Maine, Senate, Bernie Sanders, drop out

topics.title: 米奇·麦康奈尔的健康状况
topics.content: 参议院共和党领袖米奇·麦康奈尔住院三周，原因不明。一些MAGA声音要求“生命证明”，而共和党同僚称与他通话过。民主党批评共和党缺乏透明度，并提及此前国防部长奥斯汀隐瞒住院的教训。
topics.keywords: Mitch McConnell, hospitalization, proof of life, special election, transparency

topics.title: 2026年世界杯
topics.content: 世界杯八强产生，阿根廷逆转埃及，瑞士点球战胜哥伦比亚，瑞士自1954年以来首次进入四分之一决赛。
topics.keywords: World Cup, Argentina, Egypt, Switzerland, Colombia, quarter finals

topics.title: 第78届艾美奖提名
topics.content: 艾美奖提名公布，《The Pitt》和《Hacks》预计获得多项提名。玛莉丝卡·哈吉塔将主持颁奖礼，成为15年来首位女性主持人。
topics.keywords: Emmy Awards, nominations, Mariska Hargitay, host
